mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising PAYEMS (employment growth) over a 2-month horizon signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: expansion phase with labor market tightening
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS momentum are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: UNRATE crosses above 8.0% threshold, signaling deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears and risk-off sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reposition.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.0%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) signals weakening consumer demand and housing investment. When both series decline simultaneously over 2 months, forward-looking equity investors reduce growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when both RSAFS and HOUST decline are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO (industrial production) over a 2-month horizon signals strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, which supports corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following positive INDPRO momentum are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL (inflation) and elevated FEDFUNDS (restrictive rates) indicates monetary policy tightening cycle. When both are above historical medians, equity risk premiums expand and forward returns compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns during joint tightening signals are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: GDPC1 (real GDP) growth rate falls below 2.0% annualized, signaling sub-trend economic expansion that reduces earnings growth expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13616.2 billions of chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns during sub-2% GDP growth periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint signal of rising UNRATE and declining PAYEMS over a 2-month horizon indicates labor market deterioration that signals recession risk. When both deteriorate simultaneously, equity investors reduce growth expectations and shift to defensive positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp and PAYEMS declines > 50k persons month-over-month
regime_hint: post-shock recovery or early recession phase
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when both UNRATE rises and PAYEMS declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
